avalancheActivity.highlights: Increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches.
From origins in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place natural avalanches are possible, in particular medium-sized ones. This applies on shady slopes below approximately 2900 m, as well as on sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. On steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the weakly bonded old snow and reach large size in isolated cases.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

Backcountry tours and off-piste skiing should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow will fall in some regions. The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack below approximately 2700 m.
Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2400 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 1800 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

tendency.highlights: Wednesday: The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day.
Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. In very isolated cases these can release the weakly bonded old snow as well and reach large size. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m.

Dry avalanches can additionally be released, even by small loads in isolated cases, especially on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In some regions up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell in the last few days.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced in some case. The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little.
The snowpack is moist, in particular on sunny slopes in all altitude zones, as well as on west, north and east facing slopes below approximately 2500 m. Steep west, north and east facing slopes: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.

tendency.highlights: Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The danger of wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to rapid softening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.
Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.
On steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.

Afternoon: Some snow will fall, in particular at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.

tendency.highlights: The danger of wet avalanches will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day. Down to intermediate altitudes snow will fall in the evening.
avalancheActivity.comment:
A clear night will be followed in the early morning by quite favourable conditions mostly.
On very steep slopes individual small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

From the afternoon the weather will be partly cloudy. Above approximately 1600 m snow will fall during the night over a wide area. As a consequence of new snow and a light to moderate wind, mostly small wind slabs will form during the course of the night.

High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Some fresh snow and very particularly the mostly small wind slabs that are forming during the snowfall can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was good. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.
Below approximately 2800 m: The snowpack is fairly homogeneous and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.
Above approximately 3000 m, shady slopes: The snowpack is dry and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a crust that is strong in many cases.

As a consequence of mild temperatures and solar radiation the snowpack consolidated at the weekend. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitude, and in all aspects at low and intermediate altitudes.

Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.
In the last three days on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes individual small and medium-sized avalanches were observed.

tendency.highlights:
Wednesday: Down to 1500 m and below snow will fall until the early morning in some regions.
The new snow is bonding poorly with the old snowpack in many places.
The danger of moist snow slides and avalanches will already exist in the early morning.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Early morning: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften later than the day before. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

In the course of the day the natural activity of avalanches will gradually increase.
In particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place and on very steep shady slopes more small and medium-sized natural wet avalanches are possible.
The conditions are generally favourable for ski touring. Backcountry tours and ascents to alpine cabins should be concluded timely.
The near-surface layers of the snowpack can be released in isolated cases by winter sport participants in particular in extremely steep terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In the last two days the wind was moderate at times especially along the border with France.
The weather conditions gave rise to rapid settling of the snowpack.
This situation gave rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.
The amount of snow is subject to significant local variations.
In particular below the tree line hardly any snow is lying on southeast and southwest facing slopes.
Especially at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions there is still a very large amount of snow.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: As the temperature drops only isolated moist and wet avalanches are possible.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day. Up to intermediate altitudes rain will fall from the afternoon.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The conditions in the morning are mostly favourable.

From late morning the weather will be partly cloudy.
Up to 2000 m and above rain will fall over a wide area. On very steep slopes individual small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the moisture increases.
Backcountry tours should be started early and concluded timely.
Above approximately 1800 m snow will fall during the night in some localities.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

The rain will give rise in the afternoon to increasing moistening of the snowpack.
Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Above approximately 1600 m there is still a very large amount of snow on north facing slopes.

tendency.highlights:
Wednesday: The weather report and anticipated change in the avalanche danger are uncertain.
Little snow will fall. The avalanche danger will already be elevated in the early morning. In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation this applies in particular at high altitude.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will soften during the day. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on steep shady slopes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m.
As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2800 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2800 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 2100 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In some regions up to 15 cm of snow will fall above approximately 2000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation there will be only a slight increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Wet avalanches can in isolated cases be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies on very steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Above approximately 2500 m sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2500 m.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. The avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be reduced at times. Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to rapid softening of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.
Shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack at elevated altitudes.
On steep sunny slopes as well as below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.

Afternoon: Up to 15 cm of snow will fall above approximately 2000 m, in particular at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions.

tendency.highlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Over a wide area rain to 1500 m. As the moisture increases the avalanche prone locations will become more prevalent.
avalancheActivity.comment: Wet and gliding avalanches are possible. The avalanche danger should be investigated very thoroughly in the relevant locality. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack.

snowpackStructure.comment: Weather conditions will further soak the snowpack in many areas. Snow cover and thickness vary greatly depending on exposure and altitude, and are steadily decreasing. Over a wide area a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution. From starting zones where no previous releases have taken place gliding avalanches can reach intermediate altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects more medium-sized gliding avalanches and wet snow slides are possible especially in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack is wet. As a consequence of mild temperatures and rain up to high altitudes the snowpack can not consolidate during the next two days.

avalancheActivity.highlights: More moist snow slides and avalanches are possible. Little snow will fall from the afternoon.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The conditions in the morning are mostly favourable.

From the afternoon the weather will be cloudy. Up to 1800 m and above rain will fall in particular in the Prealps.

The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.
In particular on very steep slopes individual small and medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible as the moisture increases. Above approximately 1600 m snow will fall during the night in some regions.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was good. The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.
Below approximately 2800 m: The snowpack is fairly homogeneous and its surface has a melt-freeze crust.
High altitudes shady slopes: The snowpack is dry. Adjacent to ridgelines at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

As a consequence of mild temperatures and solar radiation the snowpack consolidated at the weekend. This applies in particular on steep sunny slopes at high altitude, and in all aspects at low and intermediate altitudes.

Below approximately 2400 m only a little snow is lying on south facing slopes.
In the last three days on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes individual small and medium-sized avalanches were observed.

tendency.highlights:
Wednesday: Some snow will fall in some localities. Down to 1700 m and below snow will fall until the early morning.
The new snow is bonding poorly with the old snowpack in some places.
The danger of moist snow slides and avalanches will already increase in the early morning, in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation in particular.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

Below approximately 2000 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.

tendency.highlights: Wednesday: The avalanche danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust only at high altitudes and will already soften in the late morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will only just freeze and will soften during the day.
In isolated cases the avalanches are small.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of mild temperatures the snowpack consolidated. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of moist and wet avalanches.

avalancheActivity.comment: Only isolated wet avalanches are possible. Caution is to be exercised on extremely steep shady slopes. Mostly avalanches are rather small.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften during the day.

tendency.highlights: Low avalanche danger will prevail. Wet snow requires caution.
